reasoning: The resolution criteria require that by EOY 2025 it is 'decisively clear' to Eliezer Yudkowsky that open-source is unbeatable — meaning closed-source models are 'far behind' or most business inference spending is on open models. Therefore, closed models are absolutely not 'far behind'. Furthermore, it is extremely unlikely that 'most' enterprise AI inference spending has shifted entirely away from the massive OpenAI (Azure) and Anthropic (AWS) enterprise APIs. Given EY's stringent judgment ('if it's hard to tell... resolves NO'), and the Manifold market prediction of 0.03, it is all but certain this will resolve NO.
